It is one of the two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the second Sunday of September. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also called the {{nihongo|Ichinomiya Daimyōjin|一宮大明神||}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Ono Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''Ame-no-shitaharu-no-Mikoto'''| 天下春命|}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu|1=Chichibu Province}} ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' * {{nihongo|'''Seoritsu-hime no Mikoto'''| 瀬織津比咩命|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]]'''| 伊弉諾尊 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]]'''| 素盞嗚尊|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''| 大己貴大神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]'''| 瓊々杵尊|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Hoori |Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]]'''| 彦火火出見尊 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Ukanomitama]]'''|倉稲魂命|}} ==History== The origins of Ono Jinja are unknown. The site of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] of Musashi Province are located nearby, and the shrine first appears in the historical record in 772, followed by a mention in the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in 884, and in the [[Engishiki]], where it is listed as a minor shrine. It is styled as the "ichinomiya" of Musashi in the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' and in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} [[Shintōshū]].The shrine was rebuilt in the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by the {{ill|Later Hōjō clan|en|Later Hōjō clan|WD=Q431928|lt=Later Hōjō clan|1=Later Hōjō clan}} and [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] and received a stipend in the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was rated as a county shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is located a six-minute walk from [[:en:Seiseki-Sakuragaoka Station|Seiseki-Sakuragaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Keio Electric Railway|Keio Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keio Line|Keio Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== 小野神社 (多摩市) 本殿.JPG|Honden 小野神社 (多摩市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden' 小野神社 (多摩市) 随神門.JPG|Zuishin-mon ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category-inline|Ono-jinja (Tama)}} *[http://onojinja.or.jp Official homepage] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Ono Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ono-jinja (Tama)}} [[ja:小野神社 (多摩市)]] [[zh:小野神社 (多摩市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11464224}} [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
